judgment.summary: JNJ maintains high margins and strong cash flows despite a significant recent contraction in earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 2.70 beat the 2.68 estimate by 0.9%, with the next earnings date scheduled for 2026-07-15.
positives: Forward P/E 17.84 vs trailing 26.24 — suggests a more attractive valuation on a forward basis.
Gross margin 68.0% — indicates strong fundamental profitability.
Return on equity 26.4% — demonstrates efficient management of shareholder capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) $12.51B — provides substantial liquidity for the business.
negatives: Earnings growth -52.9% — shows a massive contraction in profitability.
Quarterly earnings growth -52.4% — reflects a sharp recent decline in earnings.
Quick ratio 0.69 — indicates potential short-term liquidity constraints.
PEG ratio 2.95 — suggests the stock is priced high relative to its growth.

news.paragraph: Johnson & Johnson is navigating a changing landscape involving new drug developments and ongoing legal complexities. Traders should monitor clinical trial data regarding JNJ-1900 for stage 3 inoperable lung cancer as a potential product catalyst. The stock has also experienced a 3.5% decline since its most recent earnings report.
